---
title: "One File, Two Outputs: Syncing Your Quarto About Page with Your GitHub Profile README"
author:
  - name: Guillaume Gilles
  - name: Claude Opus 4.6
    url: https://www.anthropic.com/claude
    roles:
      - implementation
      - writing - review & editing
abstract: |
  How to use Quarto's conditional content blocks to maintain a single
  `about.qmd` that renders as both a rich website page and a clean GitHub
  profile README — with automatic sync on every build.
date: 2026-03-24
categories: [Quarto, GitHub, Dev Tools, POSSE]
---

## The Problem

If you maintain a personal website **and** a
[GitHub profile README](https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/setting-up-and-managing-your-github-profile/customizing-your-profile/managing-your-profile-readme),
you've probably felt the pain of keeping them in sync. Update your bio on
one, forget the other. Add a new certification here, miss it there.

My website is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org), and my about page
uses the beautiful
[Trestles template](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-about.html#templates)
with columns, callout blocks, and iframes — features that don't exist in
GitHub-flavored Markdown. So I used to maintain two separate files.

**Not anymore.** A single `about.qmd` now produces both outputs, and a
post-render script pushes the README to my profile repo automatically.

## The Approach

The trick relies on three Quarto features:

1. **`.content-visible when-format="..."`** — conditional content blocks
   that only render for a specific output format
2. **Multi-format rendering** — the same `.qmd` can be rendered to HTML
   *and* GFM (GitHub-Flavored Markdown)
3. **Post-render scripts** — custom scripts that run after `quarto render`

### One gotcha: format names

When rendering to GFM, you might expect `when-format="gfm"` to work.
It doesn't! Quarto internally maps GFM to the `commonmark` Pandoc
writer, and `.content-visible` matches against the **format family**.
The correct value is:

```yaml
when-format="markdown"   # ✅ works for GFM
when-format="gfm"        # ❌ does not match
```

## Structuring `about.qmd`

The file has three types of content:

### 1. HTML-only (website)

Wrap everything that uses Quarto-specific features — the Trestles hero
section, column layouts, callout blocks, and iframes:

```markdown
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

::: {#hero-heading}
# Your headline here
> Your tagline
:::

::::: {.columns}
<!-- two-column layout -->
:::::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Sponsor section with iframe
:::

:::
```

### 2. GFM-only (GitHub profile)

Add a centered header with badge links and a profile table — things that
look great on GitHub but would clash with the Trestles template:

```markdown
::: {.content-visible when-format="markdown"}

<div align="center">
<img src="https://yoursite.dev/assets/profile.png" width="150" />

### Your Name

[![Website](https://img.shields.io/badge/Website-yoursite.dev-blue)](https://yoursite.dev)
</div>

---

| Platform | Profile | Focus |
|----------|---------|-------|
| **HackTheBox** | [user](https://...) | Pentesting |

:::
```

### 3. Shared content (both)

Everything outside `.content-visible` blocks renders in **both**
formats. This is where you put the content you actually want to keep in
sync:

```markdown
<!-- No wrapper — appears everywhere -->

Today, I'm immersed in **cybersecurity**...

## 🛠️ Tech Stack
## 💼 Experience
## 🎓 Education & Certifications
```

## The Post-Render Script

Create `scripts/update-profile-readme.sh`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Prevent infinite recursion: the GFM render triggers
# another post-render, which would call this script again.
if [ "${PROFILE_README_RUNNING:-}" = "1" ]; then
  exit 0
fi
export PROFILE_README_RUNNING=1

REPO="your-username/your-username"
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
TMPDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT

# 1. Render GFM
quarto render "$PROJECT_DIR/about.qmd" --to gfm \
  --output README.md --output-dir "$TMPDIR" 2>/dev/null

# 2. Clean up Quarto artifacts
sed -i '1{/^# About$/d}' "$TMPDIR/README.md"
sed -i '1{/^$/d}' "$TMPDIR/README.md"
sed -i 's/logoColor=fff\.png/logoColor=fff/g' "$TMPDIR/README.md"

# 3. Clone, diff, push
git clone --depth 1 "git@github.com:${REPO}.git" "$TMPDIR/repo"

if ! diff -q "$TMPDIR/README.md" "$TMPDIR/repo/README.md" &>/dev/null; then
  cp "$TMPDIR/README.md" "$TMPDIR/repo/README.md"
  cd "$TMPDIR/repo"
  git add README.md
  git commit -m "chore: sync profile README from website"
  git push
fi
```

Two cleanup steps are needed because Quarto:

- Injects a `# About` title from the YAML `title:` field
- Appends `.png` to shields.io badge URLs via `default-image-extension`

### The recursion guard

This is the most critical part. When the post-render script calls
`quarto render about.qmd --to gfm`, Quarto sees it as a project render
and triggers the post-render hook **again** — creating an infinite loop.
The `PROFILE_README_RUNNING` environment variable breaks the cycle.

## Wiring It Up

Add the post-render hook to `_quarto.yml`:

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
  post-render:
    - scripts/update-profile-readme.sh
```

Make the script executable:

```bash
chmod +x scripts/update-profile-readme.sh
```

## The Result

Now every `quarto render` does double duty:

1. **Website** → `docs/about.html` with the full Trestles layout,
   columns, callouts, and embedded iframes
2. **GitHub README** → auto-pushed to `your-username/your-username` as
   a clean GFM file with badge links and a profile table

Edit once, publish everywhere.
